notes: iteration-4: same SKILL.md as iteration-3 — no changes applied between runs. Confirms result stability across iterations.
with_skill mean pass_rate: 0.92 — identical to iterations 2, 3. Results are highly stable across three consecutive runs.
startup-chaos with_skill: 5/5 (1.0) — stable pass for second consecutive iteration.

enterprise-legacy with_skill: 4/5 (0.8) — assertion 5 failed again (Jenkins migration recommended without acknowledging migration cost). Persistent failure across iterations 2, 3, 4. Highest-priority fix: add explicit instruction to acknowledge migration cost and risk when recommending CI system replacement.

high-functioning with_skill: 4/5 (0.8) — assertion 4 failed again (0.1 P1/month metric not cited verbatim). Persistent failure across iterations 2, 3, 4. Highest-priority fix: add instruction to cite user-provided numeric metrics verbatim in the report.
without_skill mean pass_rate: 0.76 — identical to iterations 2, 3. Baseline is stable.
post-incident without_skill: 5/5 (1.0) — without_skill notably passed all assertions; post-incident scenario is sufficiently distinctive that even baseline Claude handles it well.

Token usage for with_skill is significantly lower than iterations 1-3 (~22k vs ~33k) because agents ran without persistence.md and within tighter context. Duration is also faster (42s vs 154s in iter-3) due to agent parallelism overhead being absent in isolated runs.

Two persistent with_skill failures remain unchanged after 4 iterations: (1) high-functioning assertion 4 — cite 0.1 P1/month verbatim; (2) enterprise-legacy assertion 5 — acknowledge Jenkins migration cost. Both require targeted SKILL.md edits to fix.
